positive_feedback: IN1: cross-deck-consistency.js now treats structure.md, index.html, styles.css, and scripts.js as one fail-closed required-input set. The Node acceptance removes index.html/styles.css/scripts.js from a deck and confirms three inputs errors with each filename, while SKILL, R1, and R2 name the same four-file contract and prohibit excluding the incomplete deck.

OUT1: vendor/tests/test-cross-deck-consistency.js executes the real checker against two-deck fixtures, detects injected shared-spec drift, rem residue, external URL contamination, and required-input omissions, rejects an unknown category with exit 2, and requires the clean control to return PASS with totalIssues=0.

The responsibility split is now exact across R1, R2, and cross-deck-consistency-rules: the machine layer supplies C1-C2/C11-C13/C15, Agent A supplies C3-C5, Agent B supplies C6-C10, and Agent C supplies C11-C15. C2 describes the implemented numeric-rem detector instead of claiming px detection.

The skill remains a concise read-only router. Detailed execution lives in the R1/R2 prompts and rule reference; the cross-deck-reviewer adapter resolves the R2 prompt; the manifest resolves checker, agent, reference, and report schema resources; all corrections remain inside the owning skill and vendor checker/test boundary.

notes: Independent remediation review after the prior bounded INCOMPLETE cycle. Evidence: Node cross-deck acceptance PASS; slide-report-generator focused pytest 131 PASS; validate-frontmatter and prompt-contract drift checks exit 0; all four prior approval findings were checked directly against current prompt/reference/implementation/test text. No force-pass or copied prior verdict.
